title: Cross-Cultural Interface Patterns
summary: Comparative study of 24 digital products used across Indian and European contexts — cataloguing interaction patterns that translate, fail, or transform across cultures.
findings: Privacy expectations differ by collectivist/individualist context
insights: Cultural UX requires co-design with both contexts
Motion semantics carry cultural weight
Language switching is an interaction, not a setting

title: Motion & Trust Signals
summary: Testing how animation timing and easing affect perceived trustworthiness in financial and health interfaces — 40 participants across 6 motion patterns.
findings: Fast motion (<150ms) increases anxiety in financial contexts
insights: Trust is temporal — motion timing is a trust signal
Context determines optimal motion speed
Skeleton screens outperform spinners for perceived speed

title: Transit Ethnography · Zürich
summary: Ethnographic fieldwork with 32 international commuters navigating Zürich's public transit — mapping emotional journey breakpoints.
findings: Transfer moments cause 3× anxiety vs. boarding
insights: Service design must map emotion, not just tasks
Multilingual ≠ multicultural
Recovery paths matter more than prevention

personas.name: International student
personas.quote: I plan my route the night before like an exam.

title: Haptic Language Prototypes
summary: Arduino-based experiments mapping emotional meaning to haptic patterns — exploring vibration as an interaction medium.
findings: Rhythm communicates urgency better than intensity
insights: Haptics are an underused design material
Accessibility applications are immediate
Cultural haptic norms exist but are understudied

title: Scroll-Driven Narrative Architecture
summary: Framework for designing scroll interactions where content transformation carries semantic meaning — tested across 5 portfolio and editorial sites.
findings: Parallax depth aids orientation when subtle
Section transitions need 600-800ms for narrative pacing
insights: Scroll is a timeline — design it as one
Motion without meaning is decoration
Reduced motion paths are essential

title: Sonic Wayfinding Systems
summary: Conceptual framework for audio-first navigation in exhibition and public spaces — when the ear leads, the eye follows.
findings: Sound creates presence without visual load
insights: Sonic UX is an emerging discipline
Web Audio API enables rapid prototyping
Curators are untapped sonic designers

title: Oral History in Digital Archives
summary: Research into how families document, store, and lose oral histories in digital formats — auto-ethnographic study with 3 families.
findings: Generational gap in digital preservation habits
insights: Personal motivation drives preservation
Non-linear archives match memory structure
Voice carries irreplaceable emotional data

title: Touch Gesture Vocabulary
summary: Cataloguing touch gesture patterns across 15 mobile apps — proposing a design language balancing discoverability and efficiency.
findings: Standard gestures vary by cultural context
insights: Gesture design is cultural design
Onboarding is part of the gesture
Fallbacks must always exist
